summary: I ran the repository's standard test and lint commands from a clean environment (installed gems locally into `vendor/bundle` since the global Homebrew gem path wasn't writable, then used the existing, separate `pipeliner_test*` databases via `RAILS_ENV=test` — no development/production database commands were touched). `bin/rubocop` passed cleanly (115 files, no offenses). However, `bin/rails test` cannot run at all: two test files added this iteration for the live-status feature — `test/services/pipelines/broadcast_status_test.rb` and `test/services/phases/manager_tick_test.rb` — each do `include Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper`, but that constant is never actually loaded in this app (turbo-rails only requires it once `ActionCable::Server::Base` loads, which never happens here — no `app/channels`, no `mount ActionCable.server`, eager loading off in test). I verified this is a real, reproducible `NameError` and not a fluke of my setup, and confirmed the rest of the suite (96 tests across all other files) passes when run without those two files. Since Minitest loads every test file before running any, this NameError aborts the *entire* suite. I wrote `verdict: "needs_work"` with two blocker findings pointing at these two files, noting the codebase's existing working pattern (`assert_enqueued_with(job: Turbo::Streams::ActionBroadcastJob)` in `test/services/step_runs/broadcast_card_test.rb`) that avoids this dependency, and overwrote a stale `verdict.json` left over from an earlier iteration that had incorrectly claimed a pass.
